description_long: This object consists of two dark, military-style helmets placed side by side. Both helmets are made of metal with a matte, slightly weathered finish, showing signs of wear and subtle rust patches. The helmet on the left features a brown leather chin strap with a metal buckle, while the right helmet lacks a visible strap. The domed shape and flared lower rim are characteristic of mid-20th-century combat helmets, providing coverage for the head and partial protection for the sides and back of the neck. The surface is largely unadorned, with no visible logos, graphics, or ventilation holes. The overall appearance suggests durability and utilitarian design, suitable for personal protection in urban or combat environments.
description: A pair of dark, weathered metal helmets with a utilitarian, military design; one features a brown leather chin strap, both have a matte, unadorned finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both helmets side by side; the left helmet has a visible chin strap, while the right helmet does not.
description_view_1: Side/rear view highlighting the brown leather strap and buckle on the left helmet, with the right helmet's smooth back visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the full extent of the leather strap on the left helmet and the plain, rounded back of the right helmet.
description_view_3: Opposite side/rear view, both helmets' backs are visible, with the left helmet's strap partially seen and the right helmet remaining plain.
